dimensions.evidence: Source change adds case-insensitive lookup to getTagDef via toLowerCase() on both sides. Tests verify uppercase, mixed-case, and lowercase inputs. Without the plan text, the change appears focused and complete for a case-insensitivity fix.
Single line changed in tags.ts (adding .toLowerCase() calls). Only new tests added to tags.test.ts — no deletions or unrelated edits. No other files touched.

All new tests genuinely exercise the case-insensitive behavior: 'PROSODY', 'Break', 'emphasis', 'nonexistent', and isTagSupportedByProvider cross-check. No existing tests were removed or weakened; no hardcoded expected values to force a pass.

Change is a standard toLowerCase() case-insensitive comparison. Both operands are guaranteed strings by TypeScript types. No risky side effects, no new dependencies, no regression surface beyond the targeted function.
